Three-wheelers experienced the sharpest growth, indicating a strong recovery. Electric vehicle penetration is rising, though internal combustion engines still dominate. The data reflects widespread expansion across all vehicle segments.

items.title: Aurobindo Pharma arm CuraTeQ gets Health Canada nod for biosimilar
items.summary: Aurobindo Pharma announced a significant development today. Its subsidiary, CuraTeQ Biologics, has received a compliance notice from Health Canada for its cancer drug Bevqolva. This approval marks a crucial step for the bevacizumab biosimilar. Bevqolva is used to treat various cancers. Health Canada's nod confirms its safety and efficacy. This opens new avenues for cancer treatment in Canada.

items.title: India, U.K. discuss ways to boost bilateral trade
items.summary: The bilateral trade was discussed during a virtual meeting between Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and U.K. Secretary of State for Business and Trade Peter Kyle

items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium faces a hefty Rs 233.11 crore demand for unauthorized water extraction. The company allegedly drew large volumes of water daily from Bheden river without permission. This includes base charges, a six-time penalty, and monthly interest. The Burla irrigation division has issued a one-month deadline for payment.

items.title: India, UAE trade pact helps boost bilateral trade to cross $100 billion: Goyal
items.summary: Bilateral merchandise trade has grown strongly, crossing $100 billion, with services trade also rising steadily, opening new markets and greater value,” said Goyal in a social media post

items.title: GIFT City treasury hub picks up pace; 6 listed companies seek licences
items.summary: GIFT City is gaining traction as a destination for multinational companies looking to streamline their treasury operations. Recent applications for corporate treasury centre licenses highlight a growing interest in this financial hub, offering regulatory incentives and a robust framework.

items.title: Coal India's production in April drops amid rising power demand
items.summary: April brought unexpected challenges for Coal India, the giant behind India's coal production. The company experienced a significant 9.7 percent dip in output, stirring alarms as coal is the backbone of over 70 percent of the nation's electricity supply. With summer demand hitting unprecedented levels, the pressure on power consumption intensifies, risking shortages at thermal plants and affecting various sectors.

items.title: India's sugar exports to be 7.5-8 lakh tonnes in 2025-26 season on weak global prices: Official
items.summary: For the upcoming 2025-26 season, projections indicate that India's sugar exports may hover around a modest 7.5 to 8 lakh tonnes. This downturn is largely attributed to unfavorable global pricing dynamics, which are impeding broader shipping opportunities. Coupled with strict government-imposed quotas limiting exports, the situation has stabilized domestic consumption rates, resulting in a reduced surplus for foreign markets.

items.summary: India's transition to electric vehicles (EVs) will depend critically on how quickly it can build out a robust charging ecosystem, with infrastructure readiness emerging as the key trigger for mass adoption, according to a Nomura report.

items.title: NFRA sets up 4 separate functional divisions
items.summary: The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) is stepping up its game with the launch of four specialized divisions that will focus on monitoring, oversight, investigation, and disciplinary actions. Prompted by a recent Delhi High Court judgment regarding its authority, this strategic reorganization emphasizes independence among its units.

items.title: Hindustan Laboratories, RK Steel get SEBI nod for IPOs
items.summary: Both companies had filed their draft papers in September and received SEBI’s observations on April 27, which effectively clears them to proceed.
